# Transcrição: 3. GA- Campaign Breakdown Report Setup Troubleshooting

## [00:00 - 03:00]

So some of you may look at the report, download it, and actually see that nothing loads. And I'm going to show you one of the reasons why that actually happens. So once you've imported the report and you click into it to look at the data, you've set up your UTM tracking and ads manager and you're sending traffic, and then you come in here and there's nothing to see. The simplest or the most common reason for this to happen is that your tracking parameters are not set up in the way that's recommended. By the way, that's not a problem. So these last parts of the tracking are essential to help your reporting and to make that report work. However, these first two parts might be different for some of you because you've been implementing it, you've implemented it already. So this just says, you know, the sources Facebook, some people put FB, FB ads and things like that. That's absolutely fine. Some people put ads or paid social or something like that. And by the way, CPC means cost per click. It's a very, very old school way of describing paid traffic. In fact, it's probably not even the best way. This is the way that I've been using it for many years. If you use ads or anything else like that, that's absolutely fine. And the example I just showed you, they do exactly that so that they've got exactly the same tracking parameters set up. It's one of our client sites. The only difference is that they've used ads instead of CPC. So what are you doing in that scenario? Well you go into the edit screen, which is the top right here. Or if you go into your report, it's on the right hand side here, click edit, and you make a very simple change. So here, the filter, which is already set, assuming you've imported this in says include source medium exactly as it's shown. So Facebook, forward slash CPC. That's how Google Analytics receives it. That's exactly the format that is recommended here. However, if that doesn't work for you, you can change that. So for this particular ad account, I know that it's actually Facebook ads. That's the only change I need to make. I save it, I go back into the report, and all the data will appear. So here we go. So the data is now there. So just to show you, I'll go back into custom reports, click on the campaign breakdown, and all of the data is there. So that's just a real simple fix for a potential problem. Other reasons why it might not work, that your tags are not set up. So like this is, if you've not set UTM underscore source equals, so this has to be exactly as it's shown here. This part can be what if you want. This has to be exactly as shown here, UTM medium. So this is what Google Analytics looks for. And this can be exactly what you want. So as long as that part is set up correctly, and it's represented here as well, then the report should connect to your data.

## [03:00 - 03:56]

The other reason is obviously there's another problem with your UTM tracking in ads manager, or maybe you haven't actually just sent any traffic through. So in some cases, you will see no data. The second part of this is then required to pull the campaign name, the ad set name, and the ad name so that the rest of the report works so that you get the same setup as you see in ads manager. So campaign, I can click into that and I can see my ad sets, which Google Analytics calls ad content. So just wait for Google Analytics to catch up here. And then the level down, if I want to drill down further, goes into ads. And that's the structure that we have set up specifically to mirror what's in ads manager. And by the way, if you want to use it for a different ads platform, and let's say for example, they only have campaigns and ads, there's no kind of middle section, you can do the same there as well. And you can just kind of draw down and look at your data in the same way.
